---
title: "solve"
generated: true
description: "Solve the general linear system $a x = b$."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.linalg.solve`](../nitrix-linalg-solve.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:solve -->
```python
solve(a: Float[Array, '... n n'], b: Float[Array, '...']) -> Float[Array, '...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:solve -->
Solve the general linear system $a x = b$.

Batches over any leading dimensions and is reverse-mode
differentiable.  The right-hand side may be a single vector or a
stack of column vectors; the trailing shape of the solution matches
that of `b`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:solve -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `a` | `Float` | `...` × `n` × `n` | — | Square coefficient matrix, `(..., n, n)`. |
| `b` | `Float` | `...` | — | Right-hand side: `(..., n)` (vector) or `(..., n, m)` (matrix of columns). |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...`. Solution $x$ with the same trailing shape as `b`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:solve -->
::: {.callout-note}
For symmetric-positive-definite `a` prefer [`cho_solve`](../nitrix-linalg-solve/cho_solve.qmd) -- it
is roughly twice as fast (one Cholesky plus two triangular solves
versus a full LU) and numerically better-behaved on the normal
equations.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:solve -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
